rows.description: Computus Ioannis Cary Deare Beckeris a 2do 9o Novembris 1730 ad cundem Decim seoluutam 1731
rows.notes: Page title/heading with date range. No amounts present. Text is clear and legible.
Section header 'Arreagia recepta' (Arrears received). Structural label introducing a group of entries below. No amounts.
Clear reading. '10' in pounds column, '0' in shillings, '0' in pence. Vertical column lines unambiguous. All values in valid range.

Reading: 26-12-9½d. The '9' is clear, and a small 'ob' abbreviation (half-penny marker) follows. Shillings = 12 is within valid range (0-19). Pence = 9 is within valid range (0-11). Column alignment is clean.
Clear reading. '20' in pounds column, '0' in shillings, '0' in pence. Columns well-aligned, all valid values.
Clear reading. '145' in pounds column, '0' in shillings, '0' in pence. Handwriting is legible, column positioning unambiguous.
Clear reading. '20' in pounds column, '0' in shillings, '0' in pence. Standard entry format.
Clear reading. '14' in pounds, '8' in shillings, '10' in pence. All values within valid ranges. Column lines sharp and unambiguous.
Reading: 14-8-10. Description 'Lear Eajon' (or similar) is slightly unclear due to handwriting, but amounts are clear. Pence column shows '10' (valid). Shillings and pence are unambiguous.
Clear reading. '12' in pounds, '0' in shillings, '0' in pence. Standard format, all valid values.
Reading: 14-3-3. Description slightly unclear ('Ralph Chariots Orens' or similar), but the amounts are legible. Shillings = 3 and pence = 3 both within valid ranges. Columns well-aligned.
Reading: 0-7-5. Pounds column appears empty/zero. Shillings = 7 and pence = 5 are clear and valid. Slight ambiguity in determining whether pounds is 0 or blank, but context suggests 0.
Clear reading. '10' in pounds, '9' in shillings, '11' in pence. All values in valid ranges. Column alignment is sharp and unambiguous.
Reading: 2-19-5. Shillings = 19 is within valid range (0-19). Pence = 5 is valid. Pounds = 2 is clear. Minor note: '19' shillings is at the maximum valid value, but is clearly written.
Reading: 0-15-0. Pounds column is blank/zero. Shillings = 15 is clear and valid. Pence = 0 is clear.
Clear reading. '1' in pounds, '0' in shillings, '0' in pence. Simple amounts, unambiguous column alignment.
Clear reading. '4' in pounds, '12' in shillings, '11' in pence. All values within valid ranges. Clean column alignment.

Reading: 4-1-6¾d. The '6' is followed by what appears to be '3q' or a three-farthings symbol. Pence whole = 6 is valid. Fraction = 0.75 (three farthings) is plausible given the mark. Slight uncertainty in the fraction symbol, but best reading.
Clear reading. '4' in pounds, '10' in shillings, '4' in pence. All values within valid ranges. Handwriting is legible, columns well-aligned.

Reading: 7-9-11½d. Pence = 11 with a small 'ob' (half-penny) marker following. All values valid. Description 'Brew Harie' is fairly clear; amounts are legible, though the 'ob' abbreviation is slightly condensed.
Clear reading. '6' in pounds, '0' in shillings, '0' in pence. Simple, unambiguous amounts.

Reading: 26-10-0. Description includes place name 'Merston'. Amounts are clear: '26' in pounds, '10' in shillings, '0' in pence. All values valid. Slight uncertainty in parsing the full description, but monetary amounts are unambiguous.

Reading: 5-3-4. Description 'Okedenham' (or similar) is fairly clear. Amounts: '5' in pounds, '3' in shillings, '4' in pence, all within valid ranges. Minor uncertainty in first letter of description (could be O or Q), but amounts are unambiguous.
Clear reading. '4' in pounds, '0' in shillings, '0' in pence. Description and amounts both legible. Standard format.
